annotations.label: Fiber bundle (informal)

anchor.snippet: a fiber bundle ( Commonwealth English : fibre bundle ) is a space that is locally a product space

annotations.note: The informal notion is captured by the `FiberBundle` class declared in `Mathlib/Topology/FiberBundle/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Trivial bundle (informal)

anchor.snippet: This is called a trivial bundle

annotations.note: The trivial bundle is constructed as `Bundle.Trivial.fiberBundle` (and also captured Prop-style by `IsHomeomorphicTrivialFiberBundle`).

annotations.label: Section of a bundle (informal)

anchor.snippet: is called a section of

annotations.note: Mathlib treats sections informally as terms of `∀ x, E x` (with continuity lemmas like `continuousAt_section`), but has no dedicated `Section` type for general fiber bundles.

annotations.label: Structure group (informal)

anchor.snippet: known as the structure group , acting on the fiber

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a structure group acting on the fiber of a fiber bundle; `StructureGroupoid` is for charted spaces, not bundles.

annotations.label: Fiber bundle (formal)

anchor.snippet: A fiber bundle is a 4-tuple

annotations.note: The `FiberBundle` class encodes the same data via a family `E : B → Type*` and an atlas of `Trivialization`s rather than the literal 4-tuple.

annotations.label: Local triviality condition

anchor.snippet: We require that for every

annotations.note: Local triviality is built into `FiberBundle` via the fields `trivializationAt` and `mem_baseSet_trivializationAt`.

annotations.label: Local trivialization

anchor.snippet: is called a local trivialization of the bundle

annotations.note: The `Trivialization` structure is precisely a local trivialization as an open partial homeomorphism to `B × F`.

annotations.label: Fiber over a point

anchor.snippet: is homeomorphic to

annotations.note: `FiberBundle.homeomorphAt` produces a homeomorphism between each fiber `E b` and the model fiber `F`.

annotations.label: Bundle projection is open

anchor.snippet: is an open map , since projections of products are open maps

annotations.note: `FiberBundle.isOpenMap_proj` shows that the bundle projection `π F E` is an open map.

annotations.label: Smooth fiber bundle

anchor.snippet: A smooth fiber bundle is a fiber bundle in the category of smooth manifolds

annotations.note: Mathlib has smooth vector bundles (`ContMDiffVectorBundle`) and smooth charted-space infrastructure but no generic `SmoothFiberBundle` class for non-vector smooth fiber bundles.

annotations.label: Trivial bundle

anchor.snippet: Any such fiber bundle is called a trivial bundle

annotations.note: The trivial bundle example is realized as `Bundle.Trivial.fiberBundle B F`.

annotations.label: Bundles over contractible CW-complexes are trivial

anchor.snippet: Any fiber bundle over a contractible CW-complex is trivial

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem about triviality of fiber bundles over contractible CW-complexes.

annotations.label: Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: the simplest example of a nontrivial bundle

annotations.note: The Möbius strip (and its bundle structure over `S¹`) is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: A similar nontrivial bundle is the Klein bottle

annotations.note: The Klein bottle is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covering space as fiber bundle

anchor.snippet: A covering space is a fiber bundle such that the bundle projection is a local homeomorphism

annotations.note: `IsCoveringMap` is defined via evenly covered neighborhoods, but is not stated as / connected to a `FiberBundle` instance with discrete fiber.

annotations.label: Vector bundle

anchor.snippet: called vector bundles , are those whose fibers are vector spaces

annotations.note: The `VectorBundle` class encodes fiber bundles with linear trivializations whose fibers are vector spaces.

annotations.label: Principal bundle

anchor.snippet: called principal bundles , are bundles on whose fibers a free and transitive action by a group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `PrincipalBundle` definition.

annotations.label: Associated bundle

anchor.snippet: known as the associated bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no associated-bundle construction.

annotations.label: Sphere bundle

anchor.snippet: A sphere bundle is a fiber bundle whose fiber is an n -sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `SphereBundle` definition.

annotations.label: Unit sphere bundle

anchor.snippet: one can construct the associated unit sphere bundle

annotations.note: The unit sphere bundle of a normed vector bundle is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler class of sphere bundle

anchor.snippet: A sphere bundle is partially characterized by its Euler class

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Euler class of a sphere bundle.

annotations.label: Mapping torus

anchor.snippet: the mapping torus

annotations.note: The mapping torus construction is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quotient by closed subgroup as bundle

anchor.snippet: is a fiber bundle, whose fiber is the topological space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem that `G/H → G/K` (for closed `H ≤ K`) is a fiber bundle with fiber `K/H`.

annotations.label: Lie group quotient is a bundle

anchor.snippet: is a Lie group and

annotations.note: No theorem that the quotient of a Lie group by a closed subgroup gives a (principal/fiber) bundle is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf fibration

anchor.snippet: One example of this is the Hopf fibration

annotations.note: The Hopf fibration is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed Lie subgroup quotient

anchor.snippet: More generally, if

annotations.note: The general closed-Lie-subgroup quotient as a fiber bundle is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Section (cross section)

anchor.snippet: A section (or cross section ) of a fiber bundle

annotations.note: Sections appear informally as `∀ x, E x` with auxiliary continuity lemmas, but no top-level `Section` definition exists for general fiber bundles.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: The most well-known example is the hairy ball theorem

annotations.note: The hairy ball theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local section

anchor.snippet: A local section of a fiber bundle is a continuous map

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `LocalSection` concept for general fiber bundles.

annotations.label: G-atlas

anchor.snippet: atlas for the bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a G-atlas on a fiber bundle.

annotations.label: G-bundle and structure group

anchor.snippet: A G -bundle is a fiber bundle with an equivalence class of G -atlases

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `GBundle` typeclass capturing a fiber bundle equipped with a structure group.

anchors.snippet: A G -bundle is a fiber bundle with an equivalence class of G -atlases

annotations.label: Smooth G-bundle

anchor.snippet: a G -bundle is a smooth fiber bundle where G is a Lie group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no smooth G-bundle with Lie group structure group.

annotations.label: Cocycle condition

anchor.snippet: is called the (Čech) cocycle condition

annotations.note: The cocycle condition `coordChange j k ∘ coordChange i j = coordChange i k` is a field of `FiberBundleCore`.

annotations.label: Principal G-bundle

anchor.snippet: A principal G -bundle is a G -bundle where the fiber F is a principal homogeneous space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no principal G-bundle definition.

annotations.label: Bundle map / morphism

anchor.snippet: A bundle map or bundle morphism consists of a pair of continuous

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general bundle-morphism definition between fiber bundles over possibly different bases.

anchors.snippet: A bundle map or bundle morphism consists of a pair of continuous

annotations.label: Equivariant bundle morphism

anchor.snippet: bundle morphisms are also required to be

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equivariant bundle morphism for G-bundles.

annotations.label: Bundle morphism over B

anchor.snippet: a bundle morphism over

annotations.note: No `BundleMorphism` over a fixed base is defined for general fiber bundles in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bundle isomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bundle isomorphism is a bundle map

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general `BundleIso` for fiber bundles.

annotations.label: Fibered manifold

anchor.snippet: is called a fibered manifold

annotations.note: The notion of `FiberedManifold` (smooth surjective submersion) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Submersion of compact connected manifolds is a bundle

anchor.snippet: If M and N are compact and connected

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem that a submersion between compact connected manifolds is a fiber bundle.

annotations.label: Ehresmann's fibration theorem

anchor.snippet: Another sufficient condition, due to Ehresmann

annotations.note: Ehresmann's fibration theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibration

anchor.snippet: a fibration is a mapping

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Hurewicz/Serre fibration definition (the only `Fibration` file in Mathlib is order-theoretic).

annotations.label: Homotopy lifting property of bundles

anchor.snippet: a fiber bundle always has the homotopy lifting property

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have the homotopy lifting property for fiber bundles.
